rulings.Case Title: JON DOE JR VS. CHARLES AKHIDNEOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strk Pltf'S 2nd Amd Petition And Complt, Dismissal W/ Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANTs SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, CHARLES AKHIDNEOR, FRANK PEREZ, BARBARA SMITH, LINDA MARTIN and ANDREA AGHO's Motion To Strike Plaintiff's 2nd Amended Petition And Complaint, Dismissal With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment DENIED. The Court declines to exercise its discretion to strike and dismiss the petition with prejudice under the circumstances in this case. Even though second amended petition was filed untimely, leave to amend is liberally granted. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JON DOE JR VS. CHARLES AKHIDNEOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Clarification Whether The Court Excused Doe'S Lateness In Filing The Second Amended Complaint Or Whether Defendants May File A Second Motion Seeking Dismissal For Such Lateness
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFF JON DOE's Motion For Clarification Whether The Court Excused Doe's Lateness In Filing The Second Amended Complaint Or Whether Defendants May File A Second Motion Seeking Dismissal For Such Lateness GRANTED. July 1, 2014 Order shall be amended to include the following language: "The Court did not rule on the timeliness of the Second Amended Complaint." = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JON DOE JR VS. CHARLES AKHIDNEOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, line 1. RESPONDENTs CHARLES AKHIDNEOR, FRANK PEREZ, BARBARA SMITH, SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, LINDA MARTIN and ANDREA AGHO's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Petition sufficiently alleges facts and is not uncertain. Petitioner alleges that he is disabled and such disability has been recognized by Respondent since 2004; that his disability requires accommodation in a form of live-in aid authorization; that Respondents failed to afford him all administrative hearings to which he was entitled; that the decisions rendered at the hearings that did take place were erroneous under the statutes and regulations; and that the Respondents failed to provide him with all the notices to which he was entitled. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA GEYER et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Motion And Notice Of Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Obey A Court Order Against Meridian Managment Group And Its Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, line 2. PLAINTIFFs GABRIELA GEYER, TRAVIS GEYER, GEYER, GABRIELA INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF, GEYER, TRAVIS, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL's Amended Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Obey A Court Order Against Meridian Managment Group And Its Counsel Continued to September 11, 2014 to be heard in Department 301 at 9:00 a.m. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copy of opposition to Department 301 no later than August 15, 2014 with a cover letter indicating new hearing date and department. The parties are instructed again to file their discovery motions first to be heard in Depart 301, then, if parties do not stipulate to a judge pro-tem hearing the matter, the motion will be transferred to Department 501. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARILOU D SAMSON VS. ONE WEST BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint/ (Re-Notice)
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICE LLC's Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the first and third causes of action is overruled on the grounds of statute of limitations, res judicata, and collateral estoppel. This action was filed within 3 years of August 22, 2010; moving defendant was not a party to the first state action; and a different primary right was involved in the federal action. Demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is barred by two-year statute of limitations; Plaintiff failed to allege duty and breach of duty and failed to indicate in opposition that any facts can be plead to allege duty/breach of duty. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CUBIE CRAWFORD et al VS. ALTER NATHAN BADER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANTs ALTER BADER, ARI BROWNSTONE, DOMINIC BIANCHI and SUSHIEN PANG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Wrenn v. Sutton (1944) 65 Cal.App.2d Supp. 823 is not binding and its reasoning is not applicable to the case at bar as defendant's ability to respond to the notice and/or complaint is not hampered by the lack of the language in question. Landlord's intent on complete compliance with the requirements of SFRO 37.9(a)(8) may be inferred from assertions that "owner seeks to recover possession of this unit for owner move-in in good faith, without ulterior motive, and with honest intent." Whether or not landlord has such intent or is going to comply with SFRO 37.9(a)(8) requirements if and when possession is obtained is an issue that cannot be properly addressed on demurrer. Opposing party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. = (501/REQ)
